verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: His loyalty towards Constantius and his brother.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: How with this apprehension of such a design on their part, was there not an additional probability of my praying for your Grace?

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Should I feel affection for his murderer, and entertain dislike towards you his brother who avenged his death?

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Should I remember his crime, and forget that kindness of yours which you vouchsafed to assure me by letter should remain the same towards me after your brother’s death of happy memory, as it had been during his lifetime?

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: How could I have borne to look upon the murderer?

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Must I not have thought that the blessed Prince beheld me, when I prayed for your safety?

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: For brothers are by nature mirrors of each other.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Wherefore as seeing you in him, I never should have slandered you before him; and as seeing him in you, never should I have written to his enemy, instead of praying for your safety.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Of this my witnesses are, first of all, the Lord who has heard and has given to you entire the kingdom of your forefathers: and next those persons who were present at the time, Felicissimus, who was Duke of Egypt, Rufinus, and Stephanus, the former of whom was Receiver-general, the latter, Master there; Count Asterius, and Palladius Master of the palace, Antiochus and Evagrius Official Agents.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: I had only to say, ‘Let us pray for the safety of the most religious Emperor, Constantius Augustus,’ and all the people immediately cried out with one voice, ‘O Christ send help to Constantius;’ and they continued praying thus for some time.
